reviews.review_description:
It’s ok. 

Ocean game - just move around looking the fish.  Would have been ok to just chill but the fish has a paper Mario look to them.  It’s not a wide ocean, it’s a small area to explore with three or four different type of fish swimming around. I’m guess you are suppose to explore? 

Legend of the Dragon- you have a bow stick that your suppose to hit emerald balls that are coming at you.  Why?? For points? And in waves. It doesn’t change or different stages. Eh. 

Archery- actually looks good, suppose to shoot a bow at paper targets of deer, boars, bears ect. Pretty good for target practice. But again gets boring really quick. 

I’m all not bad but not great rather but it’s free …
Explore underwater, staff fighting and archery. All play really well. Passed the Time. Nice.

So when I first got this game on the Oculus quest 2 I felt like I was in the game it made me very cool and now I'm going to try it out on the quest for 3s, but there was a little problem on the quest 2 first it was a little bit laggy when I got into it the first time so that might concluded but I still like it

The dragon game is really great, I love it. You should continue developing it and remove the other two games that don't belong here. It's really a nice surprise! Maybe you could add an effect to the stick when making multiple hits on the ball, like a flame or a light effect. For the background, it would be great to have a real landscape image or some selectable wallpaper images. If you read my message, please update me on the future developments.
